security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 45 potential issues (external_commands, weak crypto, system reconnaissance). Manual evaluation confirms these are false positives. The skill contains only markdown documentation with code block examples referencing other skills. No actual shell commands, cryptographic algorithms, or system reconnaissance code exists. This is a documentation-only skill providing RAG implementation workflow guidance.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Block Backticks
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backticks as Ruby/shell command execution. These are markdown code fences (```) used for skill references like `Use @ai-product`. No actual shell commands present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Detection
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' on skill reference lines. Manual review confirms these are skill names (ai-product, rag-engineer) with no cryptographic content.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Detection
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged lines referencing other skills (similarity-search-patterns, hybrid-search-implementation) as system reconnaissance. These are legitimate skill references for workflow integration.

content.user_title: Build RAG Systems Step by Step
content.value_statement: Implementing RAG systems requires coordinating multiple components including embeddings, vector databases, and retrieval pipelines. This workflow provides a structured approach to build production-ready RAG applications.
actual_capabilities: Guide embedding model selection based on use case requirements
Provide step-by-step vector database configuration workflow
Define chunking strategies for optimal retrieval
Structure RAG implementation into 8 manageable phases
Integrate LLM context injection with retrieved documents
Set up evaluation metrics for RAG system quality
limitations: Does not execute code or interact with external systems
Requires other referenced skills to be available (ai-product, rag-engineer, etc.)
Provides workflow guidance only, not actual implementation code
Does not include pre-built vector database configurations

use_cases.title: Building a Document Q&A System
use_cases.description: Create a knowledge-base Q&A application that retrieves relevant documents and generates answers using an LLM.

use_cases.title: Implementing Semantic Search
use_cases.description: Set up semantic search capabilities over document collections using embeddings and vector similarity.

use_cases.title: Optimizing RAG Pipeline
use_cases.description: Evaluate and improve existing RAG system performance through systematic retrieval optimization.
use_cases.target_user: ML engineers optimizing AI application accuracy

prompt_templates.title: Start RAG Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me start building a RAG system. Use @rag-implementation to guide me through the initial phases.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginning a new RAG project from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Select Embedding Model
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to choose an embedding model for my RAG system. Walk me through Phase 2 of the RAG implementation workflow.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluating embedding models for a specific domain

prompt_templates.title: Configure Vector Database
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a vector database for my RAG application. Follow the Phase 3 vector database setup workflow.
prompt_templates.scenario: Configuring Pinecone, Weaviate, or other vector DB

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate RAG System
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me evaluate my RAG system quality. Use the Phase 8 evaluation workflow to define metrics and test approach.
prompt_templates.scenario: Measuring retrieval accuracy and generation quality

output_examples.input: Help me start building a RAG system
output: Phase 1: Requirements Analysis - Define your use case (document Q&A, semantic search, etc.), identify data sources, set accuracy requirements and latency targets
Phase 2: Embedding Selection - Evaluate embedding models, test domain relevance, measure embedding quality, consider cost and latency tradeoffs
Phase 3: Vector Database Setup - Choose vector database (Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma), design schema, configure indexes, set up connections

output_examples.input: Which embedding model should I use?
output: Consider these factors: domain relevance (test with your data), embedding dimension requirements, latency constraints, and cost per 1K tokens
Common options: OpenAI text-embedding-ada-002 for general use, Cohere for multilingual, sentence-transformers for self-hosted
Recommendation: Test top 3 candidates with your actual data using retrieval accuracy as the deciding metric

best_practices: Start with Phase 1 requirements analysis before diving into technical implementation
Test retrieval quality with representative queries before integrating with LLM
Use evaluation metrics from Phase 8 to validate each phase completion
anti_patterns: Skipping requirements analysis and jumping directly to embedding selection
Using default chunking without considering document structure and query patterns
Integrating LLM before verifying retrieval quality with simple tests

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. This is a workflow guide that provides guidance and references to other skills. It does not run any code or interact with external systems.

faq.question: What other skills does this reference?
faq.answer: The workflow references skills like ai-product, rag-engineer, embedding-strategies, vector-database-engineer, similarity-search-patterns, and llm-evaluation.

faq.question: Which vector databases are supported?
faq.answer: The workflow is database-agnostic. It provides guidance for setting up any vector database including Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma, Milvus, and Qdrant.

faq.question: Can I use this for production systems?
faq.answer: Yes. This workflow provides structured guidance suitable for production RAG implementations. Follow quality gates at each phase.

faq.question: How long does full implementation take?
faq.answer: Implementation time varies based on data complexity and requirements. The 8-phase structure allows incremental progress over days or weeks.

faq.question: Is this compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Use it to guide AI assistants building RAG applications.
